triageFlags.title: Credit Score Below Acceptable Threshold for Tier C
triageFlags.description: The applicant's credit score is below the minimum required for Tier B and falls into Tier C, which requires stricter criteria.
triageFlags.recommendation: Consider declining the application or requesting additional guarantees.
triageFlags.reasoning: According to the policy, a credit score of 620 or higher is required for Tier B. A score of 575 places the applicant in Tier C, necessitating stricter lending criteria.

triageFlags.title: Debt-to-Income Ratio Exceeds Allowable Threshold for Tier C
triageFlags.description: The applicant's Debt-to-Income ratio exceeds the maximum allowable for their credit tier.
triageFlags.recommendation: Decline the application as the DTI ratio indicates insufficient affordability.
triageFlags.reasoning: The policy specifies a maximum DTI of 35% for Tier C applicants. The applicant's DTI ratio exceeds this limit, indicating a potential risk of financial strain.

triageFlags.title: Vehicle Mileage Exceeds Maximum Allowed at Start of Agreement
triageFlags.description: The vehicle's mileage exceeds the maximum allowed at the start of the agreement.
triageFlags.recommendation: Decline the application due to the vehicle's non-compliance with mileage criteria.
triageFlags.reasoning: The policy states that vehicles must have a maximum mileage of 100,000 miles at the start of the agreement. The proposed vehicle exceeds this limit, making it ineligible for financing.

content.reasoning: The applicant's credit score places them in the 'Sub-Prime' tier, which imposes stricter criteria. The Debt-to-Income ratio exceeds the maximum allowable for this tier, and the vehicle's mileage surpasses the policy's limit. These factors collectively indicate a high risk level, warranting a decline.
